weather.summary: Saturday: 10°C, scattered clouds. Sunday: 10°C, overcast clouds

metadata.greeting: This weekend in London is packed with fun activities for your little one! From puppet shows at the Little Angel Theatre to family barn dances in Hackney, there's something for everyone. With the weather looking good for outdoor adventures, you can also explore London's parks and gardens.

activities.title: Family Workshops - The Everywhere Bear
activities.description: Create spoon puppet versions of the characters in The Everywhere Bear story and miniature stage backdrops.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on activity, based on a popular children's book, perfect for imaginative play.

transport.fromHackney: Bus 38 to Essex Road, then short walk.
transport.tip: Book in advance as spaces are limited.

activities.title: Cut A Shine St. Patrick's Day - Family Barn Dance
activities.description: Family barn dance with dances for the smallest kids, plus Irish stew and a kids' disco.
activities.whyItWorks: Active and fun, gets kids moving and introduces them to Irish music and dance.

transport.fromHackney: Bus 38 or 55 to Hackney Town Hall, then short walk.
transport.tip: Come hungry for the Irish stew!

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: Explore the world of creativity, play and design at the Young V&A. See fascinating objects, enjoy interactive exhibits and get inspired.
activities.whyItWorks: Free entry, interactive exhibits, lots to see and do for different age groups.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Bethnal Green.
transport.tip: Check out the sensory backpacks for younger visitors.

activities.title: Museum of the Home
activities.description: Time-travelling trip through everyday life.
activities.whyItWorks: Exhibits for kids.

transport.fromHackney: Bus 26, 55 to Hoxton.

activities.title: Geffrye Museum's Gardens
activities.description: Explore the gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: Gardens for children

transport.fromHackney: Bus 26, 55 to Hoxton.

activities.title: A Squash and a Squeeze
activities.description: Puppet show about a little old lady who thinks her house is too small.
activities.whyItWorks: Engaging puppet show based on a popular story.

transport.fromHackney: Bus 38 to Essex Road, then short walk.

activities.title: Family Day: Imagine Children's Festival
activities.description: Explore a range of free activities at the Southbank Centre's Imagine Children's Festival, including interactive installations and craft activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Free, lots of different activities, suitable for a range of ages.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then tube to Waterloo.
transport.tip: Check the schedule for specific events.

activities.title: Epping Forest
activities.description: Explore the outdoors
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor activities for all.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Epping.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, animals, and fascinating exhibits at this world-renowned museum.
activities.whyItWorks: Free entry, huge dinosaur exhibit, lots to see and do for different age groups.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Visit the Hintze Hall and see the giant blue whale skeleton.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: Visit a small zoo with wallabies, lemurs, and Scottish wildcats.
activities.whyItWorks: Free entry, animals, playground in the same area.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Northern Line to Golders Green, then short walk.
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to Hampstead Heath.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Discover how the world works with interactive exhibits and a space exploration gallery.
activities.whyItWorks: Free entry, interactive exhibits, Wonderlab (paid) for hands-on experiences.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to South Kensington.

activities.title: Changing of the Guard
activities.description: Watch the Changing of the Guard ceremony with marching bands.
activities.whyItWorks: Free, exciting for kids, bands and pageantry.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Green Park.
transport.tip: Arrive early to get a good view.

activities.title: Hyde Park
activities.description: Take a stroll along the Serpentine, look at the ducks, and explore the playgrounds.
activities.whyItWorks: Free, open space, playgrounds, nature.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Central Line to Lancaster Gate or Marble Arch.

activities.title: Queen's Walk
activities.description: Take a riverside stroll along the Queen's Walk.
activities.whyItWorks: Pedestrianised, buskers, views.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then tube to Waterloo.

activities.title: British Library - Fairy Tales
activities.description: Interactive exhibition exploring fairy tales through books, costumes, puppets, and illustrations.
activities.whyItWorks: Tactile and gently immersive, interactive elements for children to enjoy.

transport.fromHackney: Bus 38 to King's Cross, then walk.
transport.tip: Book early time slots at weekends.

activities.title: Kew Gardens - Sounds of Blossom
activities.description: Explore the magnolias and cherry blossom.
activities.whyItWorks: Beautiful spring blooms, space to roam, glasshouses for shelter.

transport.fromHackney: Tube and Train: District Line to Kew Gardens station.

activities.title: I Want My Hat Back Trilogy
activities.description: Jon Klassen's beloved books come to life on stage.
activities.whyItWorks: Puppetry.

transport.fromHackney: Bus 38 to Essex Road, then short walk.

activities.title: London Zoo's Zoonormous Easter Egg Hunt
activities.description: Search for giant Easter eggs hidden around the zoo.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines animals with an Easter egg hunt.

transport.fromHackney: Bus 253 to Camden Town, then walk.

activities.title: Hampton Court Palace - Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt
activities.description: Explore the palace grounds looking for gold bunny statues, finishing with a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Motivates kids to walk, chocolate prize at the end.

transport.fromHackney: Train from Liverpool Street to Hampton Court.

activities.title: Maritime Museum – Astronomers Take Over
activities.description: A brand new interactive space-themed adventure
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive space themed fun.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Cutty Sark.
transport.tip: Visit the Cutty Sark as well.

activities.title: Octonauts Above & Beyond
activities.description: Join the Octonauts on an exciting outdoor mission at Kew Gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: An interactive trail that invites young explorers to complete challenges, discover facts and learn how to protect our planet.

transport.fromHackney: Tube and Train: District Line to Kew Gardens station.

activities.title: Royal Mews Easter Egg Expedition
activities.description: Follow an Easter trail around the working stables at Buckingham Palace.
activities.whyItWorks: Explores carriages and courtyards.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Green Park.

activities.title: London Wetland Centre – Gozzle the gosling
activities.description: Fun-filled wetland adventure with Gozzle the gosling from Julia Donaldson and Sara Ogilvie's picture book.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive trail to discover what it takes Gozzle to become a grown-up goose.

transport.fromHackney: Tube and Bus: District Line to Hammersmith, then bus 283.

activities.title: The Lion King
activities.description: Watch Simba's adventures on the Serengeti come to life in The Lion King.
activities.whyItWorks: Stirring puppetry and live music.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Central Line to Covent Garden.
transport.tip: If you're buying tickets for the stalls, go for aisle seats to get a good view as the cast weaves down the aisles mid-performance.
